enrichment.summary: Kyle documents two days of life in Boulder in May 2014. He was rained out of work both days, spent time on his website, and found and purchased a 2003 Kawasaki KLX 400 dual-sport motorcycle from Craigslist for $2,900 using a line of credit. He ends the vlog showing the bike in his garage.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Excited about the deal and looking forward to hitting trails, despite having almost no motorcycle experience
Loves it — considers it perfect now that he has an indoor garage with the bike
Comfortable drawing the full $5,000 since he's working and the interest is the same either way

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He got up and had to work at eight on May 7, 2014.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He rode his bike through a bike path he had never used before.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He rode home on another bike path he had never used before.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The rainy weather caused his job to be canceled, so he came home and worked on his website all day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He finished one page of his website and is working on some big news related to it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He didn't work the next day either because they were supposed to get rained out, but it didn't rain.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He looked on Craigslist for a motorcycle and found one just three blocks from his house.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He rode his bike to see a 2003 Kawasaki XTR 400 in really good condition, listed for $3,100.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He went to the bank to transfer his line of credit to buy the motorcycle.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There was confusion with the bank due to another deal under the same name.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He negotiated with the seller, initially offering $2,600, then $2,700, but settled on $2,900.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He brought the motorcycle home and stored it in his garage.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The motorcycle came with trail gears and original gears.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He can't take his driver's test until May 5th.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The minimum line of credit was $5,000, so he took out $3,000 for the bike and plans to take out the remaining $2,000 for his friend.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He now has his entire $5,000 line of credit allocated, with $5,000 going to his friend.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is happy with his living situation, especially having an indoor garage to store the motorcycle.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His van doesn't fit in the garage because of the racks.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His roommate offered to help him extract something on Sunday, which they plan to film for a YouTube video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He needs to go to the bank to get more cash for his friend.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is waiting for an insurance agent to email him so he can register the bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has the manual to study for the written test, which he plans to take soon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has almost no experience riding motorcycles but is excited to hit the trails.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The motorcycle is a Kawasaki KLX 400 in pristine condition with two sets of gears.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The motorcycle has features like breakaway mirrors, lights, and signals.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He also owns kayaks but doesn't dream about them.
